objective: Survive breakfast without being pulled into open resistance
opposition: Martha's pressure, Carrow theatrics, and Owen's instinct to stay unremarkable
turn: Owen pockets the obsolete Binns edition instead of returning it
consequence_cost: He begins the day by hiding in scholarship rather than solidarity

objective: Finish detention with minimum pain while stealing reading time
opposition: Shattered stacks, Pince's patrols, and the risk of Amycus wandering through
turn: Owen finds the impossible footnote to North Stacks C-13 in the old edition
consequence_cost: His attention shifts from the school year's surface miseries to a private line of inquiry

objective: Test whether the footnote points to a real place
opposition: Curfew, broken shelving, and the danger of being caught in the Restricted Section
turn: Exposed stone numerals show the modern stacks sit over an older arrangement
consequence_cost: Owen commits to investigating the hidden location instead of letting the clue go

objective: Translate the obsolete citation into a current route
opposition: Modern catalog charms, missing maps, and curfew pressure
turn: An old prefect guide ties North Stacks C-13 to a retired shelf-chase above the kitchen runs
consequence_cost: Owen now has a physical access plan that depends on timing and his body size

objective: Reach the lost shelf and recover whatever the footnote names
opposition: A cramped crawlspace, lead-lined concealment, and ward nausea from the coffer
turn: Owen opens the lead box and finds the Grinlow Leaves with pages that describe the First Art
consequence_cost: His hand is burned, his wand misfires, and he now carries evidence too dangerous to explain easily

objective: Get help from the Room of Requirement group before the discovery overwhelms him
opposition: The resistance's immediate operational needs and Owen's own inability to frame the problem socially
turn: Neville dismisses the pitch, but Prue notices the citation Owen quotes cannot exist
consequence_cost: Owen is publicly humiliated and privately marked by the only person smart enough to hear the real anomaly

objective: Extract one usable exercise from the manuscript
opposition: Conditional text, blank pages, and Owen's impatience
turn: The pages answer differently once his burned hand touches the margin
consequence_cost: Owen realizes the manuscript is reacting to him and not simply being read

objective: Perform the smallest possible amendment on a dead vine
opposition: Imprecise instructions and no intuitive feel for how First Art propagates
turn: He changes the local condition of open instead of affecting the vine alone
consequence_cost: Latches all through the greenhouse answer the rule-change and chaos starts

objective: Contain the fallout before anyone notices
opposition: Cascading hinge failures, startled students, and approaching patrols
turn: Owen sees that the amendment has acted on category rather than target
consequence_cost: He understands the work is structurally dangerous even when the effect looks ridiculous

objective: Pass through the evening as if nothing has happened
opposition: A deepening palm burn and a wand that no longer obeys simple charms
turn: His basic school magic stutters in public
consequence_cost: Owen accepts that the First Art is real and already changing what ordinary magic feels like in his hands

objective: Find out how much Prue actually knows about the footnote
opposition: Prue's precise questions and Owen's need to protect the manuscript
turn: Prue proves the citation belongs to a censored printing lineage
consequence_cost: Keeping her outside the work becomes less rational than letting her in

objective: Use Prue's overlays to surface more of the manuscript without losing control
opposition: Cold, secrecy, and mutual mistrust
turn: Her astronomy transparencies pull the first ward tables into legible form
consequence_cost: Owen gains a collaborator and loses the possibility of pretending this is a private hobby

objective: Get through a student roundup without exposing the manuscript
opposition: Searches, frightened students, and Martha's demand for honesty
turn: Owen hides a prepared chalk line instead of joining the open protest
consequence_cost: He enters the punishment scene already divided between human duty and the work

objective: Keep Martha from taking the full force of Amycus's lesson
opposition: Public cruelty, incomplete technique, and no safe time to think
turn: Owen amends contact just long enough for curses to glance and skid
consequence_cost: The save is partial and visible, leaving magical and social residue

objective: Understand what the failed-improvised intervention has done to him
opposition: Wand recoil, ward hum, and the sense of being observed
turn: The castle answers the amendment like a struck bell
consequence_cost: Snape and the deeper sites now have reason to notice Owen specifically

objective: Confirm whether the manuscript's larger claims are materially true
opposition: Tallow's taboo, the pain of the route, and the possibility of finding nothing
turn: The East Undercroft reveals the anchor stone and the star-table of the Meridian ward system
consequence_cost: The cosmic threat becomes factual, and the school war shrinks in Owen's hierarchy of concern

objective: Rest in a room that is not being used for planning or punishment
opposition: Absence at the tables, bad news in the air, and Owen's divided attention
turn: The kitchen meal briefly restores ordinary rhythm and tenderness
consequence_cost: The contrast makes later emotional failure sharper

objective: Answer Martha's fear in a way that keeps the friendship human
opposition: Owen's inability to speak at the scale of present danger and his growing abstraction
turn: He responds with distance instead of companionship
consequence_cost: Martha understands that he is becoming harder to reach even when he is standing beside her

objective: Join the corridor sabotage without revealing his real motive
opposition: The DA needs commitment, not half-attention, and Owen wants the route for himself
turn: Martha interprets his agreement as proof he is finally showing up
consequence_cost: His private agenda is now tied to a group operation that can be harmed by his secrecy

objective: Plant the diversion and slip toward the undercroft access
opposition: Patrol timing, jittery students, and a corridor full of competing objectives
turn: Owen uses a threshold amendment to help himself, but it catches every wrong opening
consequence_cost: The plan becomes unstable before anyone knows why

objective: Stop the sabotage from becoming total farce
opposition: Armor suits, toilets, doors, and panicked students all behaving by the amended condition
turn: The corridor erupts into comic, dangerous misalignment
consequence_cost: Younger students are exposed and the operation is no longer recoverable

objective: Take responsibility or preserve the secret
opposition: Amycus's retaliation, Martha's arrest, and Owen's fear of losing the work
turn: Owen stays silent while Martha is taken for punishment
consequence_cost: The resistance stops reading him as merely useless and starts reading him as unsafe

objective: Decode the manuscript far enough to understand the full ward problem
opposition: Prue's mistrust, exhaustion, and text that only stabilizes under exact handling
turn: Together they map four failing anchors and Prue realizes he may refuse to use future power locally
consequence_cost: Owen gains method and loses moral cover with the only person still close to him

objective: Reach Snape's office without panicking
opposition: Castle surveillance, rumor, and Owen's fear that the manuscript has already betrayed him
turn: The summons itself confirms the ward scars have been noticed
consequence_cost: Owen enters the interview knowing secrecy has already partially failed

objective: Leave the office with both the manuscript and his freedom
opposition: Snape's partial knowledge, precision, and capacity for coercion
turn: Snape names pre-wand amendments and warns that the castle answers old work
consequence_cost: Owen learns he is not the only mind in Hogwarts with any vocabulary for what he has found

objective: Contain the social damage after being seen with Snape
opposition: Neville's suspicion and the optics of private access to the headmaster
turn: Neville treats the meeting as evidence of compromise
consequence_cost: Owen loses still more standing with the only organized resistance inside the school

objective: Stay on schedule for the anchor work despite Martha's detention
opposition: Martha's immediate danger and the existence of a prepared amendment that could help
turn: Owen decides not to spend the time, strength, or ward stability to intervene
consequence_cost: His ethics cross from evasive abstraction into chosen refusal

objective: Explain himself afterward or at least survive being seen
opposition: Martha's injuries and her clarity about what he has done
turn: She ends the friendship in terms that strip away his excuses
consequence_cost: Owen loses the person most committed to dragging him back to human proportion

objective: Find out exactly what is failing beyond the anchor stone
opposition: Flood-cold descent, a damaged star-lens, and the terror of being right
turn: The Departure Chamber shows that battle-scale magic at Hogwarts will widen the ward crack
consequence_cost: Owen's private project becomes a time-sensitive emergency with the coming battle as trigger

objective: Pass through an ordinary spring school day without surrendering to sentiment
opposition: The persistence of homework, growth, and routine beside occupation
turn: Normality feels newly vivid rather than trivial
consequence_cost: Owen is reminded what kind of life his choices are eroding

objective: Keep Prue beside him without lying outright
opposition: Prue's demand for a humanly proportioned compromise and Owen's inability to make one
turn: Their almost-tender closeness breaks on the fact that he still ranks the war as secondary
consequence_cost: Owen loses his last intimate ally

objective: Get battle-contingency route information from Tallow
opposition: Tallow's contempt for cleverness without care and his loyalty to the house rather than Owen
turn: Tallow gives the route but withdraws easy warmth
consequence_cost: Owen gains access and feels the social cost of how he has used people

objective: Decide whether to climb back toward ordinary loyalties
opposition: Memory of Martha and Prue pulling against the urgency of the ward crisis
turn: Owen chooses the work even in private where no one is watching
consequence_cost: From this point his isolation is self-authored, not merely imposed

objective: Read Harry's return correctly fast enough to act
opposition: Hope, orders, rumor, and the castle's rapid mobilization
turn: Owen understands the coming battle as a ward event before he understands it as a war event
consequence_cost: He separates himself inwardly from everyone else's definition of the night

objective: Reach the undercroft before the battle's magical load peaks
opposition: Prue's direct appeal, the tide of defenders moving the other way, and the shame of being seen leaving
turn: Owen keeps going past her instead of turning toward the fight
consequence_cost: His desertion becomes explicit and witnessed

objective: Prepare the anchor chamber for a working larger than anything he has attempted
opposition: Smoke, vibration, exhaustion, and the battle hammering the stone above
turn: The battle itself makes the node visible enough to work on
consequence_cost: The ritual window opens only because Hogwarts is under catastrophic stress

objective: Hold the First Art long enough to patch the ward seam
opposition: Blood loss, collapsing notation, and backlash through the castle
turn: Owen stabilizes one edge of the breach and uncovers a latent local combat clause
consequence_cost: He suddenly has a second valid use for the same hard-won authority

objective: Choose where to spend the attention he has fought to gain
opposition: The audible battle above and the knowledge that he could stop it quickly
turn: Owen continues the ward repair and leaves the combat-ending clause unused
consequence_cost: He becomes morally answerable for every death the unused clause might have prevented

objective: Force a final decision once Prue reaches the chamber
opposition: Prue's knowledge of the work, falling stone, and the active ritual she cannot safely break
turn: Prue sees the dormant clause and understands that Owen is withholding salvation
consequence_cost: The choice is no longer private, and the war ends above by other hands while he stays below

objective: Re-enter the school after the battle as if ordinary categories still apply
opposition: Bodies, smoke, exhaustion, and people who know enough to judge him
turn: Victory reorganizes the castle around other heroes and other griefs
consequence_cost: Owen discovers survival offers no social absolution

objective: Decide whether the hidden work ends with the war
opposition: Loneliness, partial success, and the knowledge that the crack is only patched
turn: Owen recommits to the undercroft and the Grinlow Leaves' secret labor
consequence_cost: His future narrows into a custodial vocation that costs him ordinary belonging
